input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition on `$(MSBuildProjectName)` (or a similar convention). In `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**Why this works:** `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated before `PackageReference` items are resolved, so you can't condition on packages there. A naming convention (`.Tests`, `.UnitTests`, etc.) is the standard approach.

**If your test projects don't follow a single suffix**, broaden the condition:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.Contains('Test'))">
```

**Alternative — custom property:** If naming conventions aren't reliable, define a property in each test `.csproj`:

```xml
<!-- In each test csproj -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Then in `Directory.Build.targets` (not `.props` — it's imported *after* the project file, so it sees the property):

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

The naming-convention approach in `.props` is simplest and most common for repos that already follow `*.Tests.csproj` naming.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a straightforward MSBuild question. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of a test-related package reference or a naming convention.

The most robust approach for MSTest + MTP is to condition on whether the project references `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` or `MSTest` packages. But since `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated before package references are resolved, you typically need to use a convention like project name ending in `.Tests`.


I'm considering a few different approaches here: using a naming convention to check the project name, leveraging `Directory.Build.targets` instead since it's evaluated after package references are resolved, or setting a custom property directly in test projects. For an MTP migration, the cleanest solution is either conditioning on the MSTest package reference in `Directory.Build.targets`, or sticking with the project name convention in `Directory.Build.props`.
